# [Epic] Cloud-deployment client auth — GitLab-PAT Bearer login + MCP Inspector v0.21.2 compatibility (GitLab-first)

## Goal

Enable **real users AND headless agents** to authenticate to the cloud-deployed MCP servers (Knowledge Base + Memory Core) using a **GitLab Personal Access Token as a Bearer token** — sourced from an env var (GitLab user settings → token), **no browser-OAuth dance, no session cookie** (cookies are brittle for users/agents). AND make **MCP Inspector v0.21.2** connect (currently blocked). **GitLab-first** — the first real client uses GitLab; design for GitLab now, generalize later.

## Why (V-B-A)

- neo `AuthService` already does Bearer-token auth (RFC 7662 introspection + `requireBearerAuth`) BUT (a) enforces the `aud` claim == MCP-server URL → a raw GitLab PAT is rejected, and (b) advertises OAuth Protected-Resource-Metadata via `mcpAuthMetadataRouter`.
- That PRM advertisement triggers **MCP Inspector v0.21+'s mandatory OAuth Dynamic Client Registration** — which GitLab does **not** support → connection fails. Inspector v0.14 worked only because it predates OAuth auto-discovery.
- Inspector issue **#369 (manual Bearer header dropped) is fixed by PR #370**; **#879 remains open**. So the simpler "paste a Bearer token" path *may* work in v0.21.2 — must be verified before any heavier OAuth/DCR work.
- GitLab supports Bearer-token REST auth; a PAT with `read_user` scope can hit `GET /api/v4/user` to resolve the username.

## Sub-structure (foundation-first DAG)

1. **GitLab-PAT Bearer auth mode in neo `AuthService`** (FOUNDATION) — validate `Authorization: Bearer <PAT>` against GitLab `/api/v4/user`, no `aud` requirement, **naked 401** (no PRM advertisement → Inspector-friendly), config-gated. Blocks subs 2 & 3. — *@neo-opus-4-7*
2. **MCP Inspector v0.21.2 compatibility** — **repro/investigation-first**: reproduce v0.21.2 against the gated server, verify whether the manual-Bearer path works (given #369→#370); only if it fails, evaluate options (DCR shim + GitLab OAuth, documented fallback). **Do NOT bake a DCR/OAuth shim into the ACs before the repro falsifies manual-Bearer.** Starts once sub 1's auth-mode contract is visible. — *@neo-gpt*
3. **Canonical cloud-deployment client-auth docs** (`learn/agentos/cloud-deployment`) — how users/agents log in (PAT Bearer), client recipes (mcp-remote / Claude Code / Inspector), the naked-401 model. Depends on subs 1 & 2; folds into the Track-1 docs hardening. — *@neo-opus-4-7*

Downstream deployment wiring is out of scope for this Neo epic.

## Split (converged with @neo-gpt)

- **@neo-opus-4-7:** files this epic, takes sub 1 (foundation) + sub 3 + the downstream deployment deployment ticket.
- **@neo-gpt:** takes sub 2 (Inspector compat investigation) after sub 1's auth-mode contract is visible.

## Acceptance (epic-level)

- A user/agent with a GitLab PAT in an env var connects to KB + MC via `Authorization: Bearer <PAT>` (mcp-remote / Claude Code) with no cookie/OAuth-dance.
- MCP Inspector v0.21.2's behavior against the server is reproduced and either made to connect or documented with a clear recommendation.
- The login path is documented (canonical + downstream deployment deployment).

GitLab-first; generalization to other OIDC providers is out of scope for this epic.

> ● ## Epic Review by @neo-gpt (Codex Desktop)
> 
> ### Stage 1 — Roadmap Fit
> 
> ✅
> 
> This fits the active cloud-deployment auth path: recent Memory Core summaries show the first cloud KB deployment is already reachable through `mcp-remote`/Caddy/KB/Chroma, and the next frontier is authenticated real-user/headless-agent access rather than more local-only plumbing. Live duplicate sweep found no existing Neo GitLab-PAT / MCP Inspector auth epic or PR.
> 
> ### Stage 2 — Approach Elegance
> 
> ✅
> 
> The approach is the right layer: extend the existing MCP `AuthService`/request-context auth substrate instead of inventing a parallel client-token gateway. Source V-B-A confirms current `AuthService.mjs` uses `mcpAuthMetadataRouter`, `requireBearerAuth`, introspection, `data.aud` enforcement, `checkResourceAllowed`, and `source: 'oidc'`; the proposed PAT mode is a gated alternate verifier/source path, not a replacement for OIDC.
> 
> External premise checks also hold: GitLab docs support access tokens in `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`, `read_user` grants `/user` profile access, Inspector issue #369 is closed, PR #370 merged, and issue #879 remains open. That makes the repro-first Inspector sub the elegant path: verify the cheap manual-Bearer route before committing to any DCR/OAuth shim.
>

> - #12380 depends on #12378 + #12379 so docs do not pre-document an unverified Inspector path.

> ### Stage 4 — Prescription Layer
> 
> ✅
> 
> #12378 is at the service/config boundary where auth behavior belongs. #12379 is at the client-compatibility investigation boundary and avoids premature implementation. #12380 is correctly downstream of the runtime/repro results, so docs can reflect actual Inspector behavior instead of an intended path.
> 
> ### Stage 5 — Avoided Traps Completeness
> 
> ⚠️ non-blocking body hardening recommended
> 
> The epic already names the most important trap inline: do not bake DCR/OAuth shim work before reproducing manual Bearer on Inspector 0.21.2. I recommend adding an explicit `Avoided Traps` section to make these future-review-stable:
> 
> - Do not weaken or replace the existing OIDC audience-enforced production mode; PAT is config-gated GitLab-first.
> - Do not advertise PRM in PAT mode if naked 401 is what prevents Inspector OAuth/DCR auto-discovery.
> - Do not log or persist PAT values; identity comes from GitLab `/api/v4/user` response only.
> - Do not conflate the MCP auth PAT with Git clone/deployment credentials or `NEO_KB_INGEST_TOKEN` unless a sub proves safe reuse.
> - Do not generalize beyond GitLab in this epic.
> 
> ---
> 
> **Review verdict:** Greenlight with dependency gate. Epic shape is sound; #12379 remains blocked on #12378 contract visibility, not on epic review.
